Full Guide

14-Day East China Mountains, Granite Peaks & Danxia River Route

Huangshan - Sanqingshan - Wuyishan

This handbook is written directly for international visitors. It is designed as an actionable route guide rather than a general sightseeing introduction. You should be able to use it to choose where to stay, understand what each natural site is best known for, follow the daily route order, choose restaurants, connect public transport or private transfers, estimate budgets and use backup plans when weather, crowds or energy levels change. All prices are reference prices only. Before departure, re-check final costs and opening rules on official platforms, Trip.com, Ctrip, 12306, Dianping, hotel front desks or scenic-area ticket counters.

Basic Information

This handbook is written directly for international visitors. It is designed as an actionable route guide rather than a general sightseeing introduction. You should be able to use it to choose where to stay, understand what each natural site is best known for, follow the daily route order, choose restaurants, connect public transport or private transfers, estimate budgets and use backup plans when weather, crowds or energy levels change. All prices are reference prices only. Before departure, re-check final costs and opening rules on official platforms, Trip.com, Ctrip, 12306, Dianping, hotel front desks or scenic-area ticket counters.

Basic InformationDetails
Route ThemeThree East China landscape systems: Huangshan granite peaks and sea of clouds, Sanqingshan granite columns and high-altitude cliff walkways, and Wuyishan Danxia cliffs with the Nine-Bend Stream.
Recommended Duration14 days / 13 nights
Route Entry / ExitRecommended entry from Hangzhou or Shanghai; start the mountain section from Huangshan, continue through Shangrao / Sanqingshan, and exit from Wuyishan, Nanping, Fuzhou or Xiamen.
Suitable ForInternational visitors seeking natural scenery, mountain views, cloud seas, cliff paths, photography, light-to-moderate hiking and scenic river experiences.
Best SeasonsApril-June and September-November. Spring and early summer are good for mist and greenery; autumn is best for clear skies and colors. Summer is humid and crowded; winter can be quiet but some mountain routes may be affected by ice.
DifficultyModerate to moderately challenging. There is no major high-altitude sickness risk, but the route includes long stairs, cable cars, mountain shuttles, early starts and weather-sensitive viewpoints.
Exchange Rate UsedUSD 1 approximately RMB 7.2. USD amounts are rounded and used for budgeting only.

Designed for first-time international visitors

Route Highlights

Huangshan Sunrise
Morning Charm of Hongcun
Chengkan Bagua Village in Huizhou, Huangshan
The Elegant Peaks of Sanqingshan
Winter Snow View of Goddess Peak, Sanqingshan
Matouyan Tea Mountain in Wuyishan

1. Route Logic and City / Scenic Area Introduction

This route connects three of East China’s most distinctive natural landscapes. Huangshan is about cloud seas, ancient pines, oddly shaped granite rocks and mountain-summit viewpoints. Sanqingshan is about lighter, sharper granite peak clusters and suspended cliff walkways. Wuyishan is a lower-altitude Danxia river landscape where the best experience is to see water from the mountain and see red cliffs from a bamboo raft. Compared with western China routes, this trip has lower altitude risk, but it still requires careful planning because mountain shuttles, cable cars, rail transfers and bamboo-raft reservations all matter.

Natural Scenery Logic: Why This Route Works as a Pure Nature Route

This is not simply a route that links three famous mountains. It deliberately shows three different landscape structures: Huangshan is a high granite mountain with cloud seas and pine-covered cliffs; Sanqingshan is a refined granite peak-and-cliff-walk system with easier high-altitude loops; Wuyishan is a red sandstone Danxia valley where the river route is as important as the mountain route. For this reason, the daily plan explains not only where to go, but also what you should look for, where to stand, how deeply to explore and what to do if visibility or energy is poor.

Landscape TypeRepresentative PlacesWhat Makes It UniqueBest ActivitiesWhen Not Suitable / Backup
Huangshan granite cloud-sea landscapeShixin Peak, Beihai, Xihai Grand Canyon, Bright Summit, YupingHigh granite peaks, pine trees growing from rock cracks, strange rocks and frequent cloud or mist effects. Huangshan is the most dramatic mountain-view section of the route.Take the Yungu Cableway uphill; walk Shixin Peak for pine and rock views; enter the upper Xihai Grand Canyon from Paiyun Pavilion; watch sunset at Bright Summit or Paiyun Pavilion; see the Greeting Pine at Yuping.Avoid the Xihai deep canyon during thunderstorms. If visibility is extremely low, use Emerald Valley, hot spring area or a short Beihai-Yuping route instead.
Sanqingshan high cliff-walk granite peaksNanqing Garden, Sunshine Coast, West Coast, Sanqing Temple areaThe peak clusters are compact and elegant. Cliff walkways allow visitors to see Goddess Peak, Python Emerging from the Mountain, cloud seas and forests without constant steep climbing.Take Jinsha or Waishuangxi Cableway; visit Nanqing Garden for the two signature rocks; walk Sunshine Coast for open peak views; use West Coast for dramatic cliff paths; add Sanqing Temple or Wanshou Garden if energy is good.If afraid of heights, reduce time on the West Coast. In heavy fog, focus on Nanqing Garden short route. If cableway queues are long, reduce the day to one main loop.
Wuyishan Danxia river valleyTianyou Peak, Nine-Bend Stream, Dahongpao, Water Curtain Cave, One-Line-Sky, Tiger Roaring RockWuyishan has lower altitude but highly distinctive red sandstone cliffs. The Nine-Bend Stream winds between rock walls, tea valleys and forested slopes.Climb Tianyou Peak for the best river overview; take the Nine-Bend Stream bamboo raft; walk the Dahongpao - Rock-Bone Flower-Fragrance trail; see Water Curtain Cave; add One-Line-Sky and Tiger Roaring Rock for cave and Danxia steps.If bamboo rafting stops, prioritize Tianyou Peak and Dahongpao. In heavy rain, reduce Tiger Roaring Rock. For low-energy visitors, choose Wuyi Palace, Jade Maiden Peak viewpoint and a tea experience.
PlaceCity / Scenic Area IntroductionAccommodation and Travel Advice
Hangzhou / ShanghaiThe easiest East China gateways for international arrivals. Hangzhou has direct high-speed rail access to Huangshan North, while Shanghai can connect to Huangshan North or Shangrao.If your international flight arrives late, stay one night near the airport or railway station. If arrival time is early enough, continue to Huangshan City or Tangkou the same day.
Huangshan City / TunxiThe main urban base for Huangshan North Railway Station, Huangshan Airport, Tunxi Old Street and city hotels. Good for arrival, food and route preparation.Stay in Tunxi if arriving late or if you want easier dining. To enter Huangshan Scenic Area next morning, you still need to transfer to Tangkou / South Gate.
Tangkou / Huangshan South GateThe practical visitor base outside Huangshan Scenic Area. It is closest to the transfer center for Yungu Temple, Ciguang Pavilion and the main mountain shuttles.Stay here if you want an early start for Huangshan. Confirm before booking whether the hotel can register foreign passports and help with luggage storage.
Huangshan Mountain-top AreaThe only realistic option if you want sunrise or sunset without descending. Mountain hotels are expensive but save energy and time.Book early. Take only a small overnight backpack. Large luggage should stay in Tangkou, Huangshan North or with a driver/hotel.
Yushan / Shangrao / Sanqingshan Foot AreaYushan and Shangrao are transport bases; Sanqingshan foot hotels are useful for early cableway entry.Stay near the cableway if scenery is the priority. Stay in Shangrao or Yushan if transport and dining are more important.
Wuyishan Resort / Sangu AreaThe most convenient base for Wuyishan Scenic Area. Hotels, restaurants, transport and the Impression Dahongpao show area are nearby.Stay in Sangu Resort or near the scenic entrances for easy access to Tianyou Peak, Nine-Bend Stream, Dahongpao and Wuyi Palace.
Wuyishan North / Nanping / Fuzhou / XiamenFinal transport buffer options. Wuyishan and Nanping are closer to the scenic area; Fuzhou and Xiamen have more flight options.If departure is early, stay near the railway station or airport the night before. Do not place a long scenic walk on departure day.

2. 14-Day Route Overview

DayRouteMain ExperienceOvernight
Day 1Arrive in Hangzhou / Shanghai / HuangshanArrival, rest, payment and transport checkHangzhou / Huangshan City
Day 2Hangzhou / Shanghai - Huangshan North - TangkouHigh-speed rail to Huangshan, transfer to Tangkou or Tunxi, prepare for mountain entryTangkou / Tunxi
Day 3Huangshan Core Day 1Yungu Cableway, Shixin Peak, Beihai, Lion Peak, Paiyun Pavilion, upper Xihai Grand CanyonOn Huangshan Mountain
Day 4Huangshan Core Day 2Sunrise, Bright Summit, Aoyu Peak, Hundred-Step Cloud Ladder, Yuping, Greeting Pine, descend by Yuping CablewayTangkou / Huangshan City
Day 5Huangshan Flexible DayEmerald Valley, Hongcun / Xidi optional cultural buffer, hot spring or restHuangshan City / Tunxi
Day 6Huangshan - Shangrao / YushanRail transfer toward Sanqingshan; arrival rest and local foodShangrao / Yushan / Sanqingshan foot area
Day 7Sanqingshan Full DayNanqing Garden, Goddess Peak, Python Emerging from the Mountain, Sunshine Coast, West CoastSanqingshan / Shangrao
Day 8Sanqingshan Flexible / Transfer DayShort Sanqingshan route or transfer toward WuyishanWuyishan
Day 9Wuyishan Core Day 1Tianyou Peak, Nine-Bend Stream overview, Wuyi Palace or nearby river walkWuyishan
Day 10Wuyishan Core Day 2Nine-Bend Stream bamboo rafting, Wuyi Palace, Song Street, Jade Maiden Peak viewpointWuyishan
Day 11Wuyishan Tea Valley DayDahongpao, Rock-Bone Flower-Fragrance trail, Water Curtain Cave and tea experienceWuyishan
Day 12Wuyishan Danxia Walking DayOne-Line-Sky, Tiger Roaring Rock, Roaring Tiger Eight Views or low-energy alternativeWuyishan
Day 13Wuyishan Buffer / Departure CityRest, optional short scenic walk, transfer to Nanping / Fuzhou / Xiamen / ShanghaiDeparture city buffer
Day 14DepartureNo distant scenic area; airport or high-speed rail departure-

3. Core Price Reference Table (RMB + USD)

AreaAttraction / ItemRMBUSDNotes
HuangshanHuangshan Scenic Area entrance ticketRMB 190 / 150USD 26 / 21Common reference for regular / winter-tour pricing. Confirm the current annual policy before travel.
HuangshanYungu Cableway / Taiping Cableway one-wayRMB 80USD 11Useful for rear-mountain entry or northern access.
HuangshanYuping Cableway one-wayRMB 90USD 13Connects Ciguang Pavilion and Yuping area; good for descending after Greeting Pine.
HuangshanXihai Grand Canyon sightseeing monorailRMB 100USD 14Useful if walking the canyon one-way and saving knee energy.
HuangshanScenic shuttle between Tangkou and mountain gatesRMB 19-30USD 3-4Private cars generally cannot drive directly to Yungu Temple or Ciguang Pavilion.
Huangshan areaEmerald Valley / Hot Spring / short backup sightsRMB 80-200USD 11-28Good backup if Huangshan visibility is poor or body energy is low.
SanqingshanSanqingshan entrance ticketRMB 120-150USD 17-21Platform, season and bundle prices may vary.
SanqingshanJinsha or Waishuangxi CablewayRMB 70 up / 55 downUSD 10 / 8Common reference; confirm route and last cableway time.
WuyishanMain scenic-area ticket + shuttle packageRMB 140-235USD 19-33Depends on valid days and package rules.
WuyishanNine-Bend Stream bamboo raftRMB 130-150USD 18-21Must reserve a time slot during busy seasons; may stop in unsafe weather.
WuyishanImpression Dahongpao showRMB 198-588USD 28-82Optional evening show; not a natural scenery necessity.
IntercityHigh-speed rail segmentsRMB 80-400USD 11-56Depends on departure city, train class and date.
Price note: Tickets, cable cars, bamboo rafts and shuttle prices may vary by season, maintenance, platform bundles, holiday policies and temporary safety rules. Use this table for route budgeting, then check again by exact travel date.

4. Scenic Highlights, Activities and Backup Plan Cards

This section is the quick index for the whole nature route. If your time is limited, prioritize the “Do Not Miss” items. If weather, fog, crowds or fatigue affect the day, use the backup column directly.

Scenic SiteWhy It Is AttractiveWhat to Do InsideRecommended TimeBackup Plan
Shixin Peak / Beihai, HuangshanAncient Huangshan pines, compact granite peaks and classic cloud-sea viewpoints.Walk the Shixin Peak loop, photograph black pine silhouettes, look for Dream Brush Flower and Lion Peak area viewpoints.2-3 hrsIf crowded or foggy, shorten to Beihai - Shilin Hotel - Paiyun Pavilion.
Xihai Grand Canyon, HuangshanThe most dramatic deep-valley section on Huangshan, with steep cliffs, layered peaks and narrow paths.Enter from Paiyun Pavilion, walk the upper canyon, use the monorail if descending deeply, avoid rushing.3-5 hrsSkip in thunderstorms, icy conditions or low energy; use Bright Summit / Beihai short route.
Bright Summit / Aoyu Peak, HuangshanOne of the best high viewpoints to connect sunrise, sunset, cloud seas and mountain layers.Use as the central transition point between Beihai, Baiyun and Yuping. Good for sunset if staying nearby.1-2 hrsIf wind or fog is heavy, move slowly to the hotel or descend early.
Yuping / Greeting Pine, HuangshanThe symbol of Huangshan, with close-up pine-and-rock scenery.See Greeting Pine, walk Hundred-Step Cloud Ladder if open and energy allows, descend by Yuping Cableway.1.5-2.5 hrsIf queues are severe, descend via an alternative cableway or reverse the route.
Nanqing Garden, SanqingshanThe essential Sanqingshan section with Goddess Peak and Python Emerging from the Mountain.Follow the loop after cableway arrival; stop at major platforms instead of rushing every minor viewpoint.2.5-4 hrsIn fog, still walk the short loop because rocks may appear and disappear dramatically.
Sunshine Coast / West Coast, SanqingshanCliff walkways with open granite peak views and a lighter walking rhythm than Huangshan.Choose one or both coast routes depending on energy. Excellent for photos when clouds move fast.2-4 hrsIf afraid of heights, reduce West Coast; if cableway time is tight, do Sunshine Coast only.
Tianyou Peak, WuyishanBest high viewpoint over the Nine-Bend Stream and red Danxia river valley.Climb early, rest at platforms, look down at river bends and bamboo rafts.2.5-4 hrsIf rain or low energy, replace with Wuyi Palace, Jade Maiden Peak viewpoint or a tea-route walk.
Nine-Bend Stream Bamboo Raft, WuyishanThe signature low-altitude river experience: see red cliffs from the water.Reserve a time slot, arrive early, keep valuables dry and sit balanced on the raft.1.5-2 hrsIf rafting stops, walk Wuyi Palace + Dahongpao or climb Tianyou if weather allows.
Dahongpao - Water Curtain Cave Trail, WuyishanTea valley, rock walls and shaded walking; the best “tea landscape” section.Walk slowly through tea fields, observe cliff tea-growing areas and finish near Water Curtain Cave.2.5-4 hrsIf rain is heavy, do a shorter tea tasting and skip slippery side paths.
One-Line-Sky / Tiger Roaring Rock, WuyishanNarrow rock passages, caves, Danxia stairs and compact adventure feel.Best for active visitors who want a more physical Danxia walk after the easier raft day.2-4 hrsAvoid if claustrophobic, knee-sensitive or after heavy rain; use Wuyi Palace and tea route instead.

5. Accommodation Guide: Location, Foreign-Visitor Convenience and Reasons to Stay

DestinationRecommended StayLocationWhy RecommendedForeign-Visitor ConvenienceReference Average Price
Hangzhou / ShanghaiAirport or railway-station chain hotelsNear international arrival airport or main high-speed rail stationBest if arriving late; reduces first-night stress before mountain transfers.HighRMB 400-1,200 / USD 56-167
Huangshan City / TunxiCrowne Plaza Huangshan Yucheng / international or upper-mid hotelsTunxi urban area, near restaurants and transportGood for late arrivals and easier passport check-in; more dining choices than Tangkou.HighRMB 500-1,200 / USD 69-167
Huangshan City / TunxiOld Street / Tunxi comfort hotelsNear Tunxi Old Street or city centerConvenient for Hui cuisine and pre-mountain supplies. Confirm passport registration if using small inns.Medium-HighRMB 250-700 / USD 35-97
TangkouTransfer Center area hotelsNear Huangshan South Gate / Tangkou Transfer CenterBest for early mountain entry. Ask whether the hotel can store large luggage.Confirm before bookingRMB 250-800 / USD 35-111
Huangshan Mountain-topBaiyun Hotel / Xihai Hotel / Shilin Hotel / Beihai Hotel type staysInside Huangshan Scenic AreaRequired for convenient sunrise or sunset. Expensive and limited; book early.Medium-HighRMB 900-3,000+ / USD 125-417+
Shangrao / YushanHilton Sanqingshan Resort or similar resort hotelsNear Sanqingshan scenic area or cableway accessUseful for early cableway entry and more stable service.Medium-HighRMB 600-1,500 / USD 83-208
Sanqingshan foot areaCableway-area guesthouses / comfort hotelsNear Jinsha or Waishuangxi cablewayMost efficient for a full Sanqingshan day; confirm passport registration and breakfast time.Confirm before bookingRMB 250-900 / USD 35-125
WuyishanC&D Resort Wuyi Mountain / Wuyi Mountain Yuehua type hotelsSangu Resort or near scenic entrancesBest balance of transport, dining and scenic access; reliable for foreign visitors.HighRMB 600-1,600 / USD 83-222
WuyishanDahongpao Resort / tea-themed hotelsNear resort area or tea valleysGood for tea landscape atmosphere and slower final days.Medium-HighRMB 500-1,500 / USD 69-208
Final night cityNanping / Fuzhou / Xiamen airport or station hotelsNear departure station or airportUse as a buffer if departing early. Do not stay deep inside Wuyishan if you need an early flight.HighRMB 350-1,200 / USD 49-167
Accommodation note: International visitors normally need the original passport to check in. International brands, higher-star hotels and major-platform hotels usually handle foreign guests more smoothly. Mountain-top hotels, small Tangkou inns, Sanqingshan foot guesthouses and tea-valley stays should be contacted before payment to confirm passport check-in.

6. Dining Guide: Specific Restaurants, Dishes and Reasons to Choose

PlaceRestaurant / Food TypePlatform Rating ReferenceSpecialtyWhy RecommendedAverage Cost
Huangshan / TunxiLaojie Diyi Lou (老街第一楼)Approx. 4.2-4.6Stinky mandarin fish, tofu dishes, Hui-style braised porkClassic Huangshan Old Street restaurant; good for a first formal Hui cuisine meal.RMB 80-160 / USD 11-22
Huangshan / TunxiPiyun Huifu Cuisine (披云徽府菜)Approx. 4.2-4.7Hui-style banquet dishes, preserved pork, bamboo shootsSuitable for visitors who want a more complete regional dinner in a stable setting.RMB 100-220 / USD 14-31
Huangshan / TunxiMeishi Renjia / local Hui restaurantsApprox. 4.0-4.5Home-style Anhui dishesGood backup if Old Street restaurants are full.RMB 60-130 / USD 8-18
TangkouTangkou Hui-style local restaurants near Transfer CenterApprox. 4.0-4.5Mountain vegetables, bamboo shoots, chicken soupPractical before entering Huangshan; choose a busy restaurant near the hotel or transfer center.RMB 50-120 / USD 7-17
Huangshan MountainMountain hotel buffet / set dinnerPlatform ratings varyBuffet, simple hot dishes, noodlesThe most practical option on the mountain. Do not expect city-level dining.RMB 80-180 / USD 11-25
Huangshan snacksHuangshan sesame pastry (黄山烧饼)Approx. 4.0-4.6Crispy baked pastry with savory fillingUseful as a light snack for rail transfers or mountain preparation.RMB 5-20 / USD 1-3
Shangrao / YushanYanshan hot rice noodles (铅山烫粉) shopsApprox. 4.0-4.6Rice noodles, spicy broth, toppingsGood breakfast or quick lunch before Sanqingshan transfer.RMB 15-35 / USD 2-5
ShangraoShangrao chicken-leg shops (上饶鸡腿)Approx. 4.0-4.6Braised or roasted chicken legsFamous local snack, convenient for travel days.RMB 10-30 / USD 1-4
Sanqingshan foot areaSanqingshan local restaurants near cablewayApprox. 4.0-4.5Free-range chicken soup, bamboo shoots, local greensPractical after a mountain day; choose restaurants with visible menus and crowds.RMB 60-130 / USD 8-18
WuyishanYan Wuyi / Wuyi local cuisine restaurantsApprox. 4.2-4.7Smoked goose, red-eye fish, tea-flavored dishesGood first dinner in Wuyishan; lets visitors taste the tea-mountain cuisine style.RMB 80-180 / USD 11-25
WuyishanLangu smoked goose shops (岚谷熏鹅)Approx. 4.0-4.6Wuyishan smoked gooseSignature local flavor; spicy and smoky, best shared by a group.RMB 60-150 / USD 8-21
WuyishanWuyi Mountain Villa / resort restaurantsApprox. 4.2+Refined Fujian / tea cuisineReliable for higher-budget visitors or those who prefer calmer dining.RMB 150+ / USD 21+
WuyishanZixi rice-noodle shops (紫溪粉)Approx. 4.0-4.5Local rice noodlesSimple breakfast or lunch before scenic entry.RMB 15-40 / USD 2-6
Wuyishan tea experienceTea houses near Sangu / Dahongpao areaApprox. 4.0-4.7Wuyi rock tea tastingBest paired with the Dahongpao walking day; confirm prices before sitting down.RMB 50-300+ / USD 7-42+
Ordering guide: In Huangshan, prioritize stinky mandarin fish, Mao tofu, preserved pork with bamboo shoots, mountain vegetables and Huangshan pastry. In the Sanqingshan / Shangrao section, try Yanshan hot rice noodles, Shangrao chicken legs, local chicken soup and bamboo-shoot dishes. In Wuyishan, prioritize Langu smoked goose, red-eye fish, Zixi rice noodles, Wuyi rock tea and tea-flavored dishes. Restaurant ratings are reference ranges from public platforms such as Dianping, Ctrip and Trip.com; search the Chinese name again before departure to confirm branch status, opening hours and queues.

7. Intercity Transport, Public Transport Use and Price Table

SegmentBest OptionTimeReference PriceSuitable ForNotes
Hangzhou / Shanghai airport -> city hotelTaxi / airport hotel shuttle / metro where convenient30-90 minRMB 50-250 / USD 7-35Arrival visitorsDo not make the arrival day too complex; late arrivals should sleep near the station or airport.
Hangzhou / Shanghai -> Huangshan NorthHigh-speed rail2-4.5 hrsRMB 120-400 / USD 17-56Most visitorsBook on 12306, Trip.com or at the station with passport.
Huangshan North -> Tangkou / South GateBus / taxi / private transfer50-90 minRMB 30-250 / USD 4-35Huangshan visitorsPrivate transfer is easier with luggage; buses are cheaper but require timing checks.
Tangkou -> Huangshan mountain gatesScenic shuttle20-40 minRMB 19-30 / USD 3-4All mountain visitorsPrivate cars normally cannot go directly to Yungu Temple or Ciguang Pavilion.
Huangshan City / North Station -> Shangrao / YushanHigh-speed rail + taxi/private car2.5-5 hrsRMB 120-500 / USD 17-69Sanqingshan transferCheck whether your train arrives at Shangrao, Yushan South or another station.
Shangrao / Yushan -> Sanqingshan cableway areaTaxi / private car / tourist bus1-2 hrsRMB 50-350 / USD 7-49Sanqingshan visitorsPrivate car is recommended if carrying luggage or arriving late.
Sanqingshan -> WuyishanCar to rail station + high-speed rail + taxi3-5.5 hrsRMB 150-600 / USD 21-83Most visitorsUse Shangrao as the practical rail hub; confirm Wuyishan North / East station.
Wuyishan Resort -> scenic entrancesTaxi / hotel drop-off / scenic shuttle10-30 minRMB 20-80 / USD 3-11Wuyishan visitorsStay in Sangu Resort to reduce transfer friction.
Wuyishan -> Nanping / Fuzhou / XiamenHigh-speed rail or private transfer1-4 hrsRMB 80-500 / USD 11-69Departure visitorsChoose final city according to flight or rail departure.

8. Public Transport and Ticketing Instructions for International Visitors

High-speed rail tickets: the most reliable options are 12306 English version, Trip.com or a staffed station ticket window. Use Passport as the ID type. Carry your original passport on the travel day; station entry, security and ticket checks may all require it.

Mobile payment: Alipay is usually the most practical primary payment tool, with WeChat Pay as backup. Scenic areas, cableways, restaurants and taxis usually support QR payment, but small mountain shops and temporary drivers may still prefer cash.

On-site ticketing: if an online real-name system does not accept a foreign passport, ask the hotel front desk for help or use the staffed scenic-area ticket window. During Chinese holidays, do not rely only on same-day walk-in tickets.

Internal scenic transport: Huangshan requires scenic shuttle buses before cableways; Huangshan cableways, Sanqingshan cableways and Wuyishan shuttles / bamboo rafts all depend on same-day opening, weather and queue conditions.

Chinese information preparation: save hotel names, scenic-area names, gate names, cableway names and railway-station names in Chinese. Showing a driver Chinese addresses is more reliable than English addresses.

Luggage: for Huangshan mountain-top stays, take only a small backpack. Store large suitcases at Tangkou, Huangshan North, a hotel or with a driver. Also keep Sanqingshan and Wuyishan scenic days light.

9. Day-by-Day Detailed Travel Guide

Day 1 | Arrival in Hangzhou / Shanghai / Huangshan

ItemDetails
Today’s FocusArrival, rest, payment setup and transport check. Do not plan intensive sightseeing.
RouteArrival city -> hotel
OvernightHangzhou / Huangshan City
Estimated CostRMB 500-1,800 / USD 69-250

Recommended Route (in Suggested Order)

After arrival | Airport / rail station to hotel (30-90 minutes). Use taxi, hotel shuttle or a simple metro route only if convenient.

16:00-18:00 | Rest at the hotel and check Alipay, WeChat Pay, cash, passport, train tickets and translation app.

18:30-20:00 | Easy dinner. In Huangshan City, try Hui cuisine; in Hangzhou / Shanghai, choose a stable restaurant near the hotel.

After 20:00 | Confirm Day 2 rail station, train time, Huangshan North arrival, and the Chinese address of your Tangkou / Tunxi hotel.

Transport and Distance Notes

The goal is a smooth start, not saving money through complicated transfers.

Huangshan North Station is not the same as Huangshan Scenic Area; Tangkou still requires about 50-90 minutes by road.

Dining Suggestions for the Day

If staying in Huangshan City: Laojie Diyi Lou, Piyun Huifu Cuisine or Huangshan pastry for a light snack.

If staying in Hangzhou / Shanghai: choose a restaurant near the hotel; do not cross the city for dinner.

Accommodation Suggestions for the Day

Late arrivals: stay near the airport or rail station.

Early arrivals: continue to Huangshan City or Tangkou if the rail schedule is comfortable.

Foreign Visitor Notes

Save all Chinese addresses.

Prepare RMB 500-1,000 in cash.

Do not place your passport in checked or stored luggage.

Backup Plan

Flight delay: cancel dinner outside and rest.

Payment failure: use cash/card first and ask the hotel for help the next morning.

Day 2 | Hangzhou / Shanghai - Huangshan North - Tangkou

ItemDetails
Today’s FocusRail transfer and mountain preparation day. The key task is to reach Tangkou or Tunxi and prepare for Huangshan entry.
RouteHangzhou / Shanghai -> Huangshan North -> Tangkou / Tunxi
OvernightTangkou / Tunxi
Estimated CostRMB 700-2,200 / USD 97-306

Recommended Route (in Suggested Order)

08:00-09:00 | Breakfast, check out and go to the high-speed rail station.

09:00-12:30 | High-speed rail to Huangshan North. Actual time varies by departure city.

12:30-14:00 | Transfer from Huangshan North to Tangkou or Tunxi hotel.

15:00-17:00 | If staying in Tangkou, locate the Transfer Center and buy raincoat, trekking pole, water and snacks.

18:00-20:00 | Hui cuisine dinner and early rest.

Transport and Distance Notes

Huangshan North to Tangkou can be done by bus, taxi or private transfer. With luggage and first-time visitors, private transfer or hotel pickup is easier.

If staying in Tunxi, leave earlier on Day 3; if staying in Tangkou, you save time for early mountain entry.

Dining Suggestions for the Day

Dinner in Tangkou: choose a busy local Hui-style restaurant near the hotel or Transfer Center.

Dinner in Tunxi: Laojie Diyi Lou, Piyun Huifu Cuisine or another well-rated Hui cuisine restaurant.

Accommodation Suggestions for the Day

Tangkou is best for early mountain entry.

Tunxi is better for late arrival and restaurants but requires an earlier start next morning.

Foreign Visitor Notes

Buy rail tickets with passport and carry the original passport for station checks.

Confirm whether you will use Yungu Cableway or Yuping Cableway on Day 3.

Backup Plan

Late arrival: stay in Tunxi and start later on Day 3.

Bad weather forecast: still go to Tangkou, but prepare a shorter mountain route.

Day 3 | Huangshan Core Day 1: Yungu Cableway - Beihai - Xihai

ItemDetails
Today’s FocusFirst major mountain day: rear-mountain entry, pine-and-rock viewpoints, and upper Xihai Grand Canyon if weather and energy allow.
RouteTangkou -> Huangshan Scenic Area -> mountain-top hotel
OvernightOn Huangshan Mountain
Estimated CostRMB 1,500-4,000 / USD 208-556

Recommended Route (in Suggested Order)

06:30-07:30 | Breakfast, store large luggage and go to Tangkou Transfer Center.

07:30-09:00 | Scenic shuttle to Yungu Temple, then Yungu Cableway uphill.

09:00-11:30 | Shixin Peak and Beihai area: focus on ancient pines, stone peaks and short viewpoint loops.

11:30-12:30 | Simple lunch near Beihai / hotel area.

13:00-16:30 | Paiyun Pavilion and upper Xihai Grand Canyon. Walk only as deep as weather and knees allow.

17:00-18:30 | Sunset attempt at Paiyun Pavilion, Bright Summit or a viewpoint near your hotel.

18:30-20:00 | Mountain hotel dinner and early rest.

Transport and Distance Notes

Tangkou -> scenic shuttle -> Yungu Cableway is the easiest first entry route for most foreign visitors.

Xihai Grand Canyon has steep sections. Use the sightseeing monorail if descending deeply and saving energy.

Dining Suggestions for the Day

Lunch/dinner: mountain hotel restaurant or simple scenic-area food. Bring snacks because choices are limited and expensive.

Accommodation Suggestions for the Day

Stay at Baiyun, Xihai, Shilin, Beihai or similar mountain-top hotels according to route and sunrise plan. Book early.

Foreign Visitor Notes

Take only a small backpack.

Weather changes quickly; keep raincoat and warm layer ready.

Do not enter deep canyon routes in thunderstorm or icy conditions.

Backup Plan

Poor visibility: focus on Shixin Peak and Beihai short loop; skip Xihai deep canyon.

Low energy: go from Beihai to hotel early and save strength for Day 4.

Day 4 | Huangshan Core Day 2: Sunrise - Bright Summit - Yuping - Descend

ItemDetails
Today’s FocusSecond Huangshan day: sunrise, high ridge walking and the Greeting Pine / Yuping front-mountain exit.
RouteMountain-top hotel -> Yuping -> Tangkou / Huangshan City
OvernightTangkou / Huangshan City
Estimated CostRMB 900-2,800 / USD 125-389

Recommended Route (in Suggested Order)

05:00-07:00 | Sunrise attempt. Choose Bright Summit, Lion Peak, Refreshing Terrace or a hotel-advised viewpoint.

07:00-08:30 | Breakfast and check out from mountain hotel.

08:30-11:30 | Walk through Bright Summit / Aoyu Peak / Baiyun area depending on hotel location.

11:30-13:00 | Simple lunch or snack stop.

13:00-15:30 | Yuping area: Greeting Pine, nearby rock formations and photo stops.

15:30-17:30 | Descend by Yuping Cableway or alternative route, shuttle back to Tangkou.

18:00-20:00 | Dinner in Tangkou or Huangshan City.

Transport and Distance Notes

The mountain route is weather-sensitive. Cableway queues can be long during holidays, so do not leave descent too late.

Hundred-Step Cloud Ladder and Aoyu Peak steps can be tiring; reduce side routes if knees are sore.

Dining Suggestions for the Day

After descending, choose a proper Hui cuisine dinner in Tangkou or Tunxi. This is better than eating too much on the mountain.

Accommodation Suggestions for the Day

If continuing to Sanqingshan next day, stay in Huangshan City / Tunxi or near Huangshan North. If you want a slower recovery, stay one more night in Tangkou.

Foreign Visitor Notes

Check cableway closing time in the morning.

Do not miss last shuttle / cableway.

Keep passport and hotel booking accessible.

Backup Plan

Rain or heavy fog: descend earlier and use hot spring / Tunxi rest.

Knee pain: use cableway and skip extra peak climbs.

Day 5 | Huangshan Flexible Recovery Day

ItemDetails
Today’s FocusA buffer day after Huangshan: choose nature backup, cultural village, hot spring or rest according to weather and body condition.
RouteHuangshan area
OvernightHuangshan City / Tunxi
Estimated CostRMB 600-2,000 / USD 83-278

Recommended Route (in Suggested Order)

08:30-09:30 | Slow breakfast and energy check.

09:30-12:30 | Option A: Emerald Valley or mountain-foot scenic area for water and easier walking.

09:30-15:30 | Option B: Hongcun / Xidi if you want a cultural buffer with village scenery.

Afternoon | Option C: hot spring / café / hotel rest if legs are tired.

18:00-20:00 | Hui cuisine dinner and confirm Day 6 transfer.

Transport and Distance Notes

Use this day as a pressure-release day. Do not force another difficult hike after Huangshan.

If transferring from Tunxi to another city next day, stay near Huangshan North or city center.

Dining Suggestions for the Day

Hui cuisine: stinky mandarin fish, Mao tofu, bamboo shoots, preserved pork and Huangshan pastry.

Accommodation Suggestions for the Day

Stay in the same Tunxi / Huangshan City hotel if possible; moving hotels creates unnecessary effort.

Foreign Visitor Notes

This is a good laundry and rest day.

Confirm rail ticket to Shangrao / Yushan and the Sanqingshan hotel address.

Backup Plan

Rain: choose museum / old street / rest.

Low energy: skip all optional attractions and sleep.

Day 6 | Huangshan - Shangrao / Yushan / Sanqingshan Foot Area

ItemDetails
Today’s FocusTransfer toward Sanqingshan. The goal is to position well for an early cableway start on Day 7.
RouteHuangshan -> Shangrao / Yushan -> Sanqingshan area
OvernightShangrao / Yushan / Sanqingshan foot
Estimated CostRMB 700-2,500 / USD 97-347

Recommended Route (in Suggested Order)

08:00-09:00 | Breakfast and check out.

09:00-13:30 | High-speed rail or rail combination toward Shangrao / Yushan.

13:30-15:30 | Transfer to Sanqingshan foot hotel or stay in Shangrao / Yushan depending on schedule.

16:00-18:00 | Rest and confirm cableway entrance, ticket, weather and opening time.

18:00-20:00 | Local dinner: rice noodles, chicken soup or simple Jiangxi food.

Transport and Distance Notes

There are multiple possible rail stations. Confirm whether your train arrives at Shangrao, Yushan South or another station.

If staying near the cableway, private car from station is easier with luggage.

Dining Suggestions for the Day

Try Yanshan hot rice noodles, Shangrao chicken legs or local chicken soup.

Accommodation Suggestions for the Day

Stay near the cableway for early entry; stay in Shangrao / Yushan for transport and dining convenience.

Foreign Visitor Notes

Save the cableway name in Chinese.

Ask the hotel about earliest breakfast and taxi to the cableway.

Backup Plan

Late rail arrival: sleep in Shangrao / Yushan and start Sanqingshan later.

Bad weather: prepare a shorter Sanqingshan loop.

Day 7 | Sanqingshan Full Day

ItemDetails
Today’s FocusThe core Sanqingshan day: Nanqing Garden, signature granite rocks, cliff walkways and open peak views.
RouteSanqingshan Scenic Area
OvernightSanqingshan / Shangrao
Estimated CostRMB 900-2,800 / USD 125-389

Recommended Route (in Suggested Order)

07:00-08:00 | Breakfast and transfer to cableway station.

08:00-09:00 | Take Jinsha or Waishuangxi Cableway uphill.

09:00-12:00 | Nanqing Garden: Goddess Peak, Python Emerging from the Mountain and compact granite viewpoints.

12:00-13:00 | Simple lunch or packed food.

13:00-15:30 | Sunshine Coast walkway for open peak-sea views.

15:30-17:00 | West Coast cliff walkway if energy and weather allow.

17:00-18:00 | Cableway down and return to hotel.

18:30-20:00 | Dinner near hotel or in Shangrao / Yushan.

Transport and Distance Notes

Cableway queues can be long. Arrive early and confirm last descending time.

Do not try to walk every minor viewpoint; prioritize Nanqing Garden + one coast route.

Dining Suggestions for the Day

Lunch: bring simple food because mountain food is limited.

Dinner: choose local Jiangxi dishes, chicken soup or noodle shops near the hotel.

Accommodation Suggestions for the Day

If you will transfer to Wuyishan on Day 8, stay where next morning transport is easiest.

Foreign Visitor Notes

Sanqingshan weather can change quickly; a brief fog window can still produce excellent scenery.

Use trekking shoes; cliff walkways can be wet.

Backup Plan

Foggy day: keep Nanqing Garden short loop and wait for moving clouds.

Fear of heights: reduce West Coast and use Sunshine Coast / Nanqing Garden only.

Day 8 | Sanqingshan Flexible Morning / Transfer to Wuyishan

ItemDetails
Today’s FocusUse the morning for a short second look if weather is good, then transfer to Wuyishan.
RouteSanqingshan / Shangrao -> Wuyishan
OvernightWuyishan
Estimated CostRMB 800-2,800 / USD 111-389

Recommended Route (in Suggested Order)

Morning Option A | If weather was poor on Day 7, do a short Sanqingshan re-entry or nearby viewpoint only if logistics allow.

Morning Option B | If Day 7 was complete, rest and transfer earlier.

11:00-15:30 | Car to rail station + high-speed rail toward Wuyishan / Nanping.

15:30-17:30 | Arrive in Wuyishan, check in and confirm bamboo-raft reservation.

18:30-20:00 | Wuyishan dinner: smoked goose, red-eye fish or tea-flavored dishes.

Transport and Distance Notes

Use Shangrao as the practical rail hub. Confirm Wuyishan North / East / Nanping station before booking.

Do not schedule a full Sanqingshan day and a long transfer on the same day.

Dining Suggestions for the Day

Dinner: Wuyi local cuisine restaurant; try Langu smoked goose, red-eye fish and vegetables cooked with tea flavor.

Accommodation Suggestions for the Day

Stay in Sangu Resort or near Wuyishan scenic entrances. This saves time for Days 9-12.

Foreign Visitor Notes

Reserve the Nine-Bend Stream bamboo raft early if traveling in peak season.

Confirm scenic shuttle package validity.

Backup Plan

If rail delay occurs, skip all evening activities and only check in.

If bamboo raft slots are unavailable, swap Day 9 and Day 10 activities.

Day 9 | Wuyishan Core Day 1: Tianyou Peak and River Overview

ItemDetails
Today’s FocusStart Wuyishan from above: climb Tianyou Peak for the best view over the Nine-Bend Stream and Danxia cliffs.
RouteWuyishan Scenic Area
OvernightWuyishan
Estimated CostRMB 800-2,500 / USD 111-347

Recommended Route (in Suggested Order)

08:00-09:00 | Breakfast and go to scenic entrance.

09:00-12:30 | Tianyou Peak. Climb slowly, rest at platforms and focus on the river bends below.

12:30-13:30 | Lunch / rest.

14:00-16:00 | Wuyi Palace, Song Street or nearby river walk for a low-intensity afternoon.

16:00-17:30 | Optional Jade Maiden Peak viewpoint if weather is good.

18:00-20:00 | Dinner in Sangu Resort area.

Transport and Distance Notes

Tianyou Peak is the best “mountain looking at river” viewpoint on this route. Start early to avoid heat and crowds.

The climb has stairs; take it slowly and do not rush after several previous mountain days.

Dining Suggestions for the Day

Lunch: simple scenic-area food or return to Sangu.

Dinner: Yan Wuyi / local Wuyi cuisine restaurants, or resort restaurant for higher comfort.

Accommodation Suggestions for the Day

Continue staying in the same Wuyishan hotel to avoid moving luggage.

Foreign Visitor Notes

Carry water and sun protection.

If bamboo raft ticket is already reserved for Day 10, double-check the time tonight.

Backup Plan

Rain or low energy: reduce Tianyou climb and use Wuyi Palace + tea tasting.

If Tianyou is crowded, visit later or swap with Dahongpao trail.

Day 10 | Wuyishan Core Day 2: Nine-Bend Stream Bamboo Raft

ItemDetails
Today’s FocusThe signature Wuyishan water experience: see the red cliffs from the river.
RouteNine-Bend Stream + Wuyi Palace
OvernightWuyishan
Estimated CostRMB 900-2,600 / USD 125-361

Recommended Route (in Suggested Order)

07:30-08:30 | Breakfast and go to bamboo-raft pier according to reserved time.

Morning | Nine-Bend Stream bamboo raft (about 1.5-2 hours). Keep valuables dry and follow staff instructions.

Late morning | Arrive near Wuyi Palace / Song Street area and walk slowly.

12:30-14:00 | Lunch in Sangu or near Wuyi Palace.

14:00-16:30 | Jade Maiden Peak viewpoint or relaxed riverbank photography.

18:00-20:00 | Dinner and optional Impression Dahongpao show if interested.

Transport and Distance Notes

Bamboo raft time slots matter. Arrive early and do not miss the reserved time.

Rafting may be suspended during unsafe water or weather conditions.

Dining Suggestions for the Day

Lunch: Zixi rice noodles or local dishes.

Dinner: Langu smoked goose, red-eye fish or tea cuisine.

Accommodation Suggestions for the Day

Stay in Sangu Resort; it is convenient for dinner, show, taxi and next-day scenic entry.

Foreign Visitor Notes

Use a waterproof bag for phone, passport and wallet.

If you do the evening show, avoid an overly heavy dinner beforehand.

Backup Plan

Raft stops: replace with Tianyou Peak, Dahongpao trail or tea tasting.

Low energy: raft + Wuyi Palace only.

Day 11 | Wuyishan Tea Valley Day: Dahongpao - Water Curtain Cave

ItemDetails
Today’s FocusA slower walking day through tea landscapes, rock walls and shaded valleys.
RouteDahongpao -> Rock-Bone Flower-Fragrance Trail -> Water Curtain Cave
OvernightWuyishan
Estimated CostRMB 700-2,300 / USD 97-319

Recommended Route (in Suggested Order)

08:30-09:30 | Breakfast and transfer to Dahongpao area.

09:30-11:30 | Dahongpao mother-tree area and tea valley viewpoints.

11:30-13:00 | Lunch / tea break.

13:00-15:30 | Rock-Bone Flower-Fragrance trail toward Water Curtain Cave.

15:30-17:00 | Tea tasting or return to hotel.

18:00-20:00 | Dinner with tea-flavored dishes or local smoked goose.

Transport and Distance Notes

This is the best day to understand Wuyishan as a tea landscape, not just a mountain.

The path can be slippery in rain; wear proper shoes.

Dining Suggestions for the Day

Try tea-flavored dishes, smoked goose and rock-tea tasting. Ask prices clearly before long tea sessions.

Accommodation Suggestions for the Day

Same Wuyishan hotel. Staying in one place keeps the route comfortable.

Foreign Visitor Notes

Do not buy expensive tea without comparing prices.

Keep walking distance moderate after several mountain days.

Backup Plan

Heavy rain: do a shorter Dahongpao visit + indoor tea tasting.

Low energy: skip Water Curtain Cave and return early.

Day 12 | Wuyishan Danxia Walking Day: One-Line-Sky / Tiger Roaring Rock

ItemDetails
Today’s FocusA more physical Danxia day with narrow passages, rock stairs and compact viewpoints.
RouteOne-Line-Sky / Tiger Roaring Rock / low-energy alternative
OvernightWuyishan
Estimated CostRMB 700-2,300 / USD 97-319

Recommended Route (in Suggested Order)

08:30-09:30 | Breakfast and choose the route based on body condition.

09:30-11:30 | One-Line-Sky: narrow rock passage experience; not suitable for claustrophobic visitors.

11:30-13:00 | Lunch and rest.

13:00-16:00 | Tiger Roaring Rock / Roaring Tiger Eight Views for Danxia stairs and viewpoints.

16:00-17:30 | Return to hotel, rest and prepare for transfer day.

18:00-20:00 | Final Wuyishan dinner.

Transport and Distance Notes

This day is optional in intensity. If you are tired, do not force both One-Line-Sky and Tiger Roaring Rock.

Rock passages and steps can be wet or narrow.

Dining Suggestions for the Day

Dinner: choose a concrete Wuyishan restaurant such as a well-rated local cuisine place in Sangu, Langu smoked goose shop or a resort restaurant.

Accommodation Suggestions for the Day

Continue Wuyishan hotel; pack tonight for Day 13 transfer.

Foreign Visitor Notes

Avoid narrow cave routes if you dislike tight spaces.

Do not wear sandals on rock stairs.

Backup Plan

Low energy: replace with Wuyi Palace, Jade Maiden Peak viewpoint and tea tasting.

Rain: reduce rock stair sections and keep only easy scenic walks.

Day 13 | Wuyishan Buffer / Transfer to Departure City

ItemDetails
Today’s FocusRecovery and departure preparation day. Choose a short optional activity or move to a final transport city.
RouteWuyishan -> Nanping / Fuzhou / Xiamen / Shanghai
OvernightDeparture city buffer
Estimated CostRMB 600-2,200 / USD 83-306

Recommended Route (in Suggested Order)

08:30-10:00 | Breakfast and slow packing.

Morning Option A | Short Wuyishan walk, tea shopping or café if departure is late.

Morning Option B | Transfer earlier to Nanping / Fuzhou / Xiamen if your next-day flight is early.

Afternoon | Rail or car transfer to departure city.

Evening | Simple dinner near hotel or station.

Transport and Distance Notes

Do not plan a full scenic day before a major departure.

For international flights, staying near the airport or rail station the night before is safer.

Dining Suggestions for the Day

Choose a reliable local restaurant near the final hotel, not a distant destination restaurant.

Accommodation Suggestions for the Day

Stay near departure airport or high-speed rail station.

Foreign Visitor Notes

Check passport, tickets, charging cables and payment tools.

Leave enough time for traffic and station security.

Backup Plan

Transport delay: cancel optional activity and go directly to departure city.

Bad weather: move earlier rather than staying in the scenic area.

Day 14 | Departure Day

ItemDetails
Today’s FocusNo distant scenic area. Focus on smooth departure.
RouteDeparture city -> next destination
Overnight-
Estimated CostRMB 100-600 / USD 14-83 excluding long-distance tickets

Recommended Route (in Suggested Order)

Morning | Hotel breakfast, final packing and document check.

Depending on departure time | Optional short walk or simple meal near hotel only.

Before departure | Go to airport / railway station. Arrive early for passport checks and security.

Transport and Distance Notes

Airport / station transfer usually takes 30-90 minutes depending on city and hotel.

Confirm station name carefully; many cities have more than one rail station.

Dining Suggestions for the Day

Use hotel breakfast or a simple restaurant near the station / airport.

Accommodation Suggestions for the Day

No planned accommodation. If delayed, choose a chain hotel near the station or airport.

Foreign Visitor Notes

Do not schedule distant scenic sites.

Carry passport, rail/flight booking and payment tools together.

Backup Plan

If flight or train changes, stay near the departure hub and avoid returning to Wuyishan scenic area.

10. Three-Level Budget Estimate (14 Days, Excluding International Flights)

LevelConfigurationTotal RMB BudgetTotal USD BudgetSuitable For and Notes
BudgetRegular hotels / guesthouses, rail where possible, simple meals, fewer private carsRMB 10,500-15,500USD 1,458-2,153Suitable for young travelers or visitors with China independent-travel experience. More transfers and lower comfort.
Comfort (Recommended)Comfort hotels, mountain-top stay on Huangshan, key private transfers, local restaurantsRMB 18,000-28,000USD 2,500-3,889Best for most international visitors. Balances comfort, scenery and transport reliability.
High-EndHigh-end hotels / resorts, private transfers, better mountain hotels, selected restaurantsRMB 35,000-60,000USD 4,861-8,333Suitable for families, couples and high-budget visitors. Reduces transfer and communication pressure.
CategoryBudgetComfortHigh-End
AccommodationRMB 4,500-8,000 / USD 625-1,111RMB 9,000-16,000 / USD 1,250-2,222RMB 22,000-40,000 / USD 3,056-5,556
Intercity Transport / Local TransfersRMB 2,000-3,500 / USD 278-486RMB 4,000-7,000 / USD 556-972RMB 8,000-12,000 / USD 1,111-1,667
Tickets / Cableways / Scenic ShuttlesRMB 2,000-3,200 / USD 278-444RMB 2,500-4,000 / USD 347-556RMB 3,500-5,500 / USD 486-764
DiningRMB 1,800-3,500 / USD 250-486RMB 3,500-6,000 / USD 486-833RMB 7,000-12,000 / USD 972-1,667
Backup Cash / MiscellaneousRMB 500-1,300 / USD 69-181RMB 1,000-2,000 / USD 139-278RMB 2,000-4,000 / USD 278-556
Budget note: These are per-person reference budgets, excluding international flights, shopping and travel insurance. If two travelers share hotels and private cars, per-person costs may decrease. Chinese public holidays, summer vacation, autumn foliage periods and mountain-top hotel sell-outs can significantly raise costs.

11. Practical Tips for International Visitors

Documents and Ticketing

Carry your original passport; do not rely only on photos. Hotels, railway stations, cableway ticket checks and scenic-area real-name systems may require the physical document.

If an online ticketing system does not accept your passport, try Trip.com / Ctrip international version, ask hotel front desk staff, or use the manual ticket window.

For Huangshan mountain-top hotels, Wuyishan bamboo rafts and holiday-period cableways, confirm bookings at least 1-3 days in advance.

Payment and Cash

Use Alipay as your main payment tool and WeChat Pay as backup. Carry Visa / Mastercard and RMB 500-1,500 cash.

Mountain snack stalls, luggage help, small shops and temporary drivers may require cash.

Luggage and Gear

For Huangshan mountain-top stay, pack a small overnight backpack only. Store large luggage at Tangkou, Huangshan North, hotel or with a driver.

Essential gear: non-slip shoes, raincoat, warm layer, power bank, waterproof phone bag, trekking pole, snacks and motion-sickness medicine.

Do not wear sandals on Huangshan, Sanqingshan or Wuyishan rock stair routes.

12. Risks and Backup Plans

RiskPossible ImpactBackup Plan
Rain / ThunderstormCableways, cliff paths, Xihai Grand Canyon, Sanqingshan walkways and Wuyishan rafting may be affected.Huangshan: shorten to Beihai / Bright Summit / Yuping; Sanqingshan: short Nanqing Garden loop; Wuyishan: tea route, Wuyi Palace or indoor tea tasting.
Poor VisibilityClouds may hide major viewpoints, especially on Huangshan and Sanqingshan.Wait at major viewpoints if clouds are moving. If visibility stays poor, reduce deep walking and focus on safe routes.
Low Energy / Knee PainLong stairs and multi-day mountain walking can cause fatigue.Use cableways, avoid deep Xihai canyon, reduce Sanqingshan West Coast, and replace Wuyishan Tiger Roaring Rock with Wuyi Palace or tea tasting.
Cableway / Raft SuspensionWeather, maintenance or queues may disrupt the planned route.Keep flexible day order. If Wuyishan rafting stops, climb Tianyou Peak or walk Dahongpao. If Huangshan cableway queues are too long, descend earlier.
Ticketing FailurePassport-based online reservations may fail or holiday quotas may sell out.Use Trip.com / Ctrip, hotel assistance or manual ticket windows. Avoid relying on same-day tickets during holidays.
Transport DelayRail transfers and mountain-road connections can shift timing.Use transfer days as transfer days. Stay near departure hubs before flights. Do not combine a full scenic area with a major departure.
14-Day East China Mountains, Granite Peaks & Danxia River Route · Full Guide · Responsive web edition
